item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was creeped out by Bernie Sanders going communist - then I remembered I was reading a SCP article, and I could just downvote it (I didn't - I also stop planning to upvote it when I read that).

For the record the portrayal of Bernie Sanders as wanting to take over big industry isn't accurate; wanting to LEGISLATE them into unavoidable responsability would be.

I'm also annoyed Bernie Sanders get a one liner, while Trump got an article, but meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First time reading it, I thought it was just going to be a SCP about hyperaccelerating vehicles.

I never expected the ghost story plus a tale of large business organizations causing devastation on small town.

It's the Vengeful Ghost Story, but with social commentary
(and I have a feeling that the "nationalize" comment by Sanders was included for a reason)

Reminds me of Cars… But the cars are a vehicle for one man's wrath against the devastation of corporations (and not even touching on environmental issues… Something which I wonder if 5797 has caused a lot)

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd heard this one mentioned a few times before I finally got around to reading it, and I'll admit I was expecting this to be a pretty straight-forward haha funny sort of article- I was actually very positively surprised to see that wasn't quite the case. That's not to say this isn't pretty funny (it is) but there's also some great dialogue and a really good narrative here. I like the open-endedness of the implications in the addenda as well- there's just enough unsaid to leave me thinking about it without feeling unsatisfying or vague. Good truck worm all around, easy +1 for a fun-to-read, solidly self-contained piece!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi! Glad you liked super truck worm.

My intention with the last addendum is for it to act as a kind of stinger. Since the Foundation is paying for much of this damage, and don't know how best to contain the anomaly yet, I was trying to suggest that this might be going on for quite some time - and cost the Foundation a lot of money. Not to mention the political ramifications of an oil crisis.

I didn't delve too deeply into that since it wasn't the main theme of the piece, but the Foundation inadvertently causing some kind of public crisis is something I find really fascinating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! I'm glad you liked it overall.

Originally I posted this without the interview or addendum (so it just ended with the monster's conprocs and description), and it did not do well at all. So, I self-deleted and after consulting some of the downvoters, it was pretty clear that the lack of a narrative thread was preventing the idea from reaching its full potential.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is goofy but scary, a good combo. This is going to sound weird, but I felt like the last interview and addendum were… not needed? They were fine, well written. But I like it more when there is just zero explanation. Like, sometimes a bunch of trucks merge together to become a giant worm, that's just life. Vengeful ghost seems takes the fun out of it a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God I love it when a prompt comes together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really, really enjoyed the super truck worm, and the backstory behind the anomaly. It felt cool, unique, and elegantly simple at the same time.

However, I feel the last addendum doesn't work well as an ending. "His activities are causing gas prices to go up and destabilizing the gas industry" is such an obvious consequence of how the truck worm acts that it isn't a cool ending. The wife revealing that he started melting as soon as he was put in the ground is much more dramatic, and so putting the less interesting news report after feels anti-climactic. I think the last addendum should either be moved earlier than the interview, made into a footnote, or removed entirely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Creepily direct, with an added flavor of vague storytelling. In all, pretty good, and definitely deserving of an upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Again, your dialogue shines in this

Also, as mentioned on discord, I really quite love the last addendum and find it to be a unique way around the “final note” trope

+1

item.title: Come on and join our convoy, ain't nothin' gonna get in our way
item.content:encoded:
Had a lot of fun writing this one! Particular thanks to MalyceGraves, MrAnakinSpecter, Pedagon, and Grigori Karpin for their crit/feedback. Quite a few people read this, so apologies if I forgot anybody.

Should also mention that I asked for a prompt on Discord, to which Roget responded with "catnip for trucks." And thus my anti-capitalist truck-worm was born.

Writing music: Come on, it'd be rude not to.

Image 1:
Source: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Smashed_Semi-Truck_(2522435761).jpg

Creator: dave_7

Image 2:
Source: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:OR_38_sinkhole_(6728247335).jpg

Creator: Oregon Department of Transportation

Image 3:
Source: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Jack_Kirby_1980s_by_Marc_Hempel.jpg

Creator: Marc Hempel
